old_claim.note: Electrochemical Industry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Earlier electrodeposition experiments established the process that Wright and Elkington converted into a patent and factory technology.

source_shape.source_locator: Competing processes section: dates Wright and Elkington cyanide-electrolyte innovations and patent electroplate process to 1840 and documents factory licensing and manufacture.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Birmingham electrolyte and patent innovations made industrial electroplating viable in 1840.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The replacement follows the mechanism documented for the scoped milestone. The industrial milestone scales the already demonstrated deposition process rather than depending on a broad industry category that itself includes electroplating.

ontology_before: electroplating_industrial depended on the broad or mismatched prerequisite electrochemical_industry.
ontology_after: electroplating_industrial now depends on electroplating, which matches the directly documented mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: electroplating_industrial depends on electroplating and no longer on electrochemical_industry.
would_reject_if: A reliable source shows electrochemical_industry is indispensable while electroplating is absent from the current electroplating_industrial scope.
short_reason: The industrial milestone scales the already demonstrated deposition process rather than depending on a broad industry category that itself includes electroplating.
